title: alsine tenuifolia subsp. hybrida
intro: Alsine tenuifolia subsp. hybrida, also known as mountain sorrel or alpine sorrel, is a subspecies of the Alsine tenuifolia plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its high concentration of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other essential minerals. The plant has been used to promote health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Alsine tenuifolia subsp. hybrida can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
